mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in >65% of rate-cut months, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed +1% in >50% of triggered months despite rising unemployment, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and total nonfarm payrolls indicate a synchronized contraction in output and labor demand, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS yield positive 20d returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month revisions in retail sales reflect resilient consumer demand, which supports near-term equity prices and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive revisions in RSAFS are followed by negative 5d returns in >70% of instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during high volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPIAUCSL falls below a 0.2% monthly threshold, the Federal Reserve accelerates rate cuts, lowering discount rates and triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL drops below 0.2% MoM but 20d forward returns remain negative in >65% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for yield curve slope.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth expands corporate profit margins and equity earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no significant correlation with 60d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or positive GDP prints are followed by negative returns in >60% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with declining total employment indicates a broad-based labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 20d returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals severe supply-side contraction and weak economic activity, which triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops below -1.0% MoM but 5d forward returns are positive in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power during aggressive Fed intervention periods.
